Part B.key_evidence: Faint positive residual (r~21.0) in the difference image at the center of the cutouts, coincident within 0.34 arcsec of a PS1 source whose sgscore1=0.094 indicates a galaxy-like host with colors (g-r~0.24, r-i~0.27) typical of a galaxy. classtar=0.999 and fwhm=2.72 px suggest the detection itself is point-like. ndethist=12 over deltajd~1 day on a well-covered field (ncovhist=1573).
Part B.leading_interpretation_and_support: Leading interpretation: AGN variability. The candidate sits essentially on the nucleus of a PS1 galaxy (distpsnr1=0.34 arcsec, sgscore1~0.09), showing a low-level positive brightening consistent with typical AGN stochastic variability. Point-like morphology on top of an extended host and multiple historical detections at this well-covered position support a recurrent, nuclear variable source rather than a one-off event.
Part B.alternative_analysis: A nuclear/near-nuclear supernova cannot be excluded: the positive difference residual on a faint galaxy is also the classic SN signature, and the short deltajd (~1 day) with 12 detections could represent early rising photometry. However, without a clear offset from the host nucleus and given the faint, modest amplitude, AGN remains the more economical explanation.
